rag_info.chunk_content: New York – November 12, 2024 – Lotus Technology Inc. (“Lotus” or the “Company”), a leading global intelligent and luxury mobility provider, today announced its vehicle delivery results for the first ten months of 2024. Lotus Technology Inc. continues to achieve top-rate growth among traditional luxury car brands, delivering 8,631 vehicles in the first ten months of 2024. Europe and China continued to be the major markets for Lotus Technology, with each region contributing about 35% and 25% of the total deliveries, respectively. In October, Lotus Technology Inc. started an open beta test for its urban NOA (Navigate on Autopilot) in key cities. Certain over-the-air updates of intelligent driving, including highway assistant driving functions, were launched in Europe. As previously announced, Lotus Robotics, Lotus Technology's intelligent driving arm, provides intelligent driving solutions with global coverage to both Lotus-brand intelligent vehicles and other global leading auto conglomerates such as Lynk&Co, Farizon Auto, and a top Japanese Tier-one supplier. In October, Lotus Technology Inc. was awarded the Highly Commends honor at the Reuters Sustainability Awards 2024 in the Business Transformation category as the only awardee from the automotive industry. Note 1: Invoiced deliveries, including commissioned deliveries in the US market.

rag_info.chunk_content: New York and Singapore - January 9, 2024 – Lotus Technology Inc. (“Lotus Tech” or the “Company”), a leading global luxury electric vehicle maker, announced today that its intelligent driving arm, Lotus Robotics, will showcase numerous proprietary next-generation autonomous driving software and hardware solutions at the 2024 Consumer Electronics Show (CES) in Las Vegas, USA. The technologies on display, along with Lotus Technology Inc.’s recent receipt of approval to test conditional automated driving (“Level 3” or “L3”) functions on highways in part of China, demonstrate Lotus Technology Inc.’s progress in executing the brand’s Vision80 business transformation strategy and further advancing its global research and development prowess. The ROBO Soul, ROBO Matrix, and ROBO Galaxy software being showcased at CES provide solutions for advanced driver assistance systems, remotely assisted operation, and cloud data management respectively. The technologies have been employed in Lotus Technology Inc. vehicles, contributing to their autonomous driving capabilities. The hyper SUV Eletre and hyper GT Emeya, for example, both feature cutting-edge autonomous driving hardware, aviation-level security and redundancy including dual NVIDIA DRIVE Orin chips, as well as Lotus Technology Inc.’s industry-leading cognition module, which can intelligently identify and avoid risks on the road. Lotus Robotics is also displaying hardware that it is developing as part of efforts to accelerate the adoption of self-driving technology, including the ROBOCube intelligent street cleaner.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: Lotus Technology has not been profitable since its inception. Lotus Technology incurred a net loss of US\$1,107.3 million, US\$750.3 million, and US\$724.6 million in 2024, 2023, and 2022, respectively. In addition, Lotus Technology had negative net cash flows from operating activities of US\$848.5 million, US\$386.9 million, and US\$351.4 million in 2024, 2023, and 2022, respectively. As of December 31, 2024, 2023, and 2022, Lotus Technology's accumulated deficit was US\$2.7 billion, US\$1.6 billion, and US\$846.8 million, respectively. Lotus Technology incurred capital expenditures of US\$57.3 million, US\$213.7 million, and US\$133.3 million in 2024, 2023, and 2022, respectively. Historically, Lotus Technology relied principally on proceeds from the issuance of equity securities, exchangeable notes, convertible notes, and related party borrowings to finance its operations and business expansion. Lotus Technology will require additional liquidity to continue its operations over the next 12 months. Lotus Technology is evaluating strategies to obtain the required additional funding for future operations. These strategies may include, but are not limited to, obtaining additional loans from banks or related parties, renewing existing loans when they are due, and improving operational efficiency to grow revenues and control expenses. The feasibility of such plans is contingent upon many factors out of Lotus Technology's control and is highly uncertain and difficult to predict. Lotus Technology may also seek additional equity or debt financing in the future to satisfy capital requirements, respond to adverse developments or changes in its circumstances or unforeseen events or conditions, or fund organic or inorganic growth.

title: We have not been profitable and had negative net cash flows from operations. If we do not effectively manage our cash and other liquid financial assets, execute our plan to increase profitability and obtain additional financing, we may not be able to continue as a going concern. 
summary: The company has struggled with persistent financial losses since its establishment, reporting net losses of $1.1 billion, $750.3 million, and $724.6 million for the years 2024, 2023, and 2022, respectively. It has also faced negative cash flows from operating activities totaling $848.5 million, $386.9 million, and $351.4 million during the same period, leading to an accumulated deficit of $2.7 billion, $1.6 billion, and $846.8 million by the end of each year. Despite incurring capital expenditures of $57.3 million, $213.7 million, and $133.3 million over the three years, the company has relied heavily on various financing methods, including equity securities and related party borrowings, to sustain its operations.

To navigate the next 12 months, the company seeks additional liquidity and is considering funding strategies such as loans and enhancing operational efficiency. However, the effectiveness of these strategies is uncertain and contingent on external factors. The need for further equity or debt financing to address capital requirements or unforeseen challenges poses additional risks, as favorable financing terms are not guaranteed, potentially jeopardizing operational viability.

The company is under significant pressure to achieve positive cash flow while managing various contractual obligations, including derivative arrangements and debts. Although it plans to invest in increasing vehicle production, expanding its sales and service network, and advancing research and development, there are concerns that these investments may not yield immediate revenue growth or positive cash flow. This situation raises doubts about the company's ability to continue as a going concern, which could further restrict access to favorable financing options and increase the risk of substantial investment losses for shareholders. Additionally, the company faces significant risks that may impede its revenue generation, including insufficient demand for its vehicles, rising competition, and unexpected expenses, complicating its path to profitability.

summary: Lotus Technology Inc. has reported its financial performance for the years ending December 31, 2024, and 2023, highlighting significant changes in revenue, costs, and losses. These results indicate that while Lotus Technology experienced substantial revenue growth, it also encountered rising costs and deepening losses, reflecting ongoing challenges. All figures are in thousands of US dollars, and adjustments for recapitalization following a merger in February 2024 have been made.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: Lotus Technology’s American Depositary Shares will begin trading on the Nasdaq on February 23, 2024, under the ticker symbol “LOT.” New York and Singapore - February 20, 2024 – Lotus Technology Inc. (“Lotus Tech” or the “Company”), a leading global luxury electric vehicle maker, and L Catterton Asia Acquisition Corp (“L CAA”) (NASDAQ: LCAA), a special purpose acquisition company formed by affiliates of L Catterton, a leading global consumer-focused investment firm, announced today that their previously proposed business combination is expected to be completed on February 22, 2024. The combined company will retain Lotus Technology Inc.'s name as “Lotus Technology Inc.” and its American Depositary Shares (ADS) will commence trading on the Nasdaq under the ticker symbol “LOT” on February 23, 2024. The business combination was approved by L CAA shareholders at an extraordinary general meeting held on February 2, 2024. Integrating Lotus Technology’s sports car DNA with a new generation of premium lifestyle vehicles, Lotus Technology Inc. is committed to transforming the brand into an advanced, fully electric, intelligent, and sustainable luxury mobility provider before its 80th anniversary in 2028. Lotus Technology Inc. has already set this ambitious plan in motion by launching a leading product portfolio of high-performing battery electric vehicles (“BEVs”) ahead of other global luxury automakers. These BEVs include Eletre, a new breed of all-electric hyper-SUV powered by Lotus Technology’s proprietary 800-volt EPA, and Emeya, an all-electric hyper-GT among the most advanced vehicles of its kind in the world.

summary: Lotus Technology Inc., a luxury electric vehicle manufacturer, is poised to make its debut on the Nasdaq stock exchange on February 23, 2024, trading under the ticker symbol "LOT." This significant milestone follows the completion of its merger with L Catterton Asia Acquisition Corp (LCAA), which is set to finalize on February 22, 2024, after receiving shareholder approval on February 2, 2024. Lotus Tech aims to transform into a fully electric and sustainable luxury mobility provider by 2028, coinciding with its 80th anniversary, and plans to achieve a 100% electric product portfolio by 2027. The company has already introduced high-performing battery electric vehicles, such as the Eletre hyper-SUV and the Emeya hyper-GT, with plans for two additional models in the next two years. Since announcing the merger, Lotus Tech has secured over $880 million in financing commitments, marking one of the largest de-SPAC transactions since 2023. CEO Qingfeng Feng expressed enthusiasm for the company's growth as a public entity, while L CAA Co-CEO Chinta Bhagat highlighted their shared vision for establishing a leading luxury electric vehicle brand. To commemorate its public listing, Lotus Tech will ring the Nasdaq opening bell on February 23, an event that will be live-streamed for viewers.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: New York and Singapore - January 9, 2024 – Lotus Technology Inc. (“Lotus Tech” or the “Company”), a leading global luxury electric vehicle maker, announced today that its intelligent driving arm, Lotus Robotics, will showcase numerous proprietary next-generation autonomous driving software and hardware solutions at the 2024 Consumer Electronics Show (CES) in Las Vegas, USA. The technologies on display, along with Lotus Technology Inc.’s recent receipt of approval to test conditional automated driving (“Level 3” or “L3”) functions on highways in part of China, demonstrate Lotus Technology Inc.’s progress in executing the brand’s Vision80 business transformation strategy and further advancing its global research and development prowess. The ROBO Soul, ROBO Matrix, and ROBO Galaxy software being showcased at CES provide solutions for advanced driver assistance systems, remotely assisted operation, and cloud data management respectively. The technologies have been employed in Lotus Technology Inc. vehicles, contributing to their autonomous driving capabilities. The hyper SUV Eletre and hyper GT Emeya, for example, both feature cutting-edge autonomous driving hardware, aviation-level security and redundancy including dual NVIDIA DRIVE Orin chips, as well as Lotus Technology Inc.’s industry-leading cognition module, which can intelligently identify and avoid risks on the road. Lotus Robotics is also displaying hardware that it is developing as part of efforts to accelerate the adoption of self-driving technology, including the ROBOCube intelligent street cleaner.

metadata.title_summary:
title: Lotus Technology Showcases
Autonomous Driving Progress at CES 2024 
summary: Lotus Technology Inc., a leading luxury electric vehicle manufacturer, is set to showcase its advanced autonomous driving solutions at the 2024 Consumer Electronics Show (CES) in Las Vegas, highlighting its commitment to innovation and safety. The company's intelligent driving division, Lotus Robotics, will present proprietary software and hardware, including the ROBO Soul, ROBO Matrix, and ROBO Galaxy, which enhance driver assistance, enable remote operations, and manage cloud data. These technologies are integral to Lotus Tech's vehicles, such as the hyper SUV Eletre and hyper GT Emeya, which feature cutting-edge hardware like dual NVIDIA DRIVE Orin chips and a unique cognition module for risk management.

Recently, Lotus Tech received approval to test Level 3 automated driving on highways in Wuxi, China, a pilot zone for vehicle connectivity. This allows Eletre drivers to utilize its L3 capabilities on designated highways, supported by the vehicle's advanced Level 4-ready hardware, including a deployable LIDAR system and multiple sensors for enhanced perception. Lotus Robotics is also promoting self-driving technology through its ROBOCube intelligent street cleaner.

As part of its Vision80 business transformation strategy, Lotus Tech is enhancing its global R&D capabilities, with CEO Mr. Qingfeng Feng expressing optimism about the future of intelligent driving. The company is also nearing the completion of a merger with L Catterton Asia Acquisition Corp, expected to finalize in early 2024, which will transition Lotus Tech to a public entity listed on Nasdaq under the ticker "LOT." Visitors can explore Lotus Robotics' innovations at booth 9217 in the Tech East AI pavilion from January 9-12.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: In recognition of Lotus Technology Inc.’s leadership in intelligent driving, the Company recently received approval to test Level 3 autonomous driving in Wuxi, a city near Shanghai serving as China’s pilot zone for “internet of vehicles” connectivity. This license allows Eletre drivers to use the Eletre’s Level 3 functionality on designated highways in the city. The Eletre is equipped with state-of-the-art Level 4-ready hardware including the world’s first deployable LIDAR system, and leverages five different sensor types to supplement high-definition digital maps with live, 360° perception. “Lotus Robotics’ leading technologies bear testament to Lotus Technology Inc.’s world-class research and development innovation capabilities and signify a significant step forward in the Vision80 strategy. Lotus Technology Inc. looks forward to testing these innovative technologies on the roads in Wuxi as it strives to strengthen Lotus Technology Inc.’s intelligent driving capabilities and deliver even better, safer driving experiences to its customers around the world,” said Mr. Qingfeng Feng, Chief Executive Officer of Lotus Technology Inc. Visit Lotus Robotics at the Las Vegas Convention Center’s North Hall, in the Tech East AI (Artificial Intelligence) pavilion at booth 9217 from January 9-12. As announced in January 2023, Lotus Technology Inc. has entered into a definitive agreement and plan of merger (the “Merger Agreement”) with L Catterton Asia Acquisition Corp (“LCAA”) (NASDAQ: LCAA), a special purpose acquisition company formed by affiliates of L Catterton, a leading global consumer-focused investment firm.

title: 
summary: The financial overview of Lotus Technology Inc. highlights significant growth and challenges across various metrics for the periods ending June 30, 2024, compared to the same periods in 2023. The bar chart analysis indicates a remarkable increase in financial figures, with quarterly revenues rising from **$130 million** in 1H23 to **$398 million** in 1H24, and from **$110 million** in 2Q23 to **$225 million** in 2Q24. Deliveries also surged, with 4,873 units delivered in 1H24, marking a **239%** year-over-year increase.

Despite these positive trends in revenue and deliveries, the company faced escalating costs, leading to increased operating losses and net losses. The operating loss expanded to **$438 million** in 1H24, and the net loss reached **$460 million**, reflecting ongoing investments and operational challenges. The adjusted EBITDA also showed a decline, indicating that while revenues grew, the company is still navigating significant financial hurdles.

In terms of assets and liabilities, total assets increased from **$1.58 billion** to **$2.51 billion**, driven by growth in current assets. However, total liabilities also rose, resulting in a narrower shareholders' deficit, which improved from **$(571 million)** to **$(202 million)**.

Additionally, the company is focused on strategic growth, as evidenced by its ambitious production targets, aiming for over **30,000 units** by 2026, and a gross profit margin exceeding **20%**. The vehicle lineup includes exclusive models like the Emeya x Blossom Enchantment and the Evija x Fittipaldi, emphasizing Lotus' commitment to luxury and performance.

Overall, while Lotus Technology Inc. demonstrates robust growth potential and a strong market presence, it continues to face challenges related to rising costs and losses, necessitating careful management of its financial strategies moving forward.

In terms of assets and liabilities, total assets increased from **$1.58 billion** to **$2.51 billion**, driven by growth in current assets. However, total liabilities also rose, resulting in a narrower shareholders' deficit, which improved from **$(571 million)** to **$(202 million)**.

Additionally, the company is focused on strategic growth, as evidenced by its ambitious production targets, aiming for over **30,000 units** by 2026, and a gross profit margin exceeding **20%**. The vehicle lineup includes exclusive models like the Emeya x Blossom Enchantment and the Evija x Fittipaldi, emphasizing Lotus' commitment to luxury and performance.

Overall, while Lotus Technology Inc. demonstrates robust growth potential and a strong market presence, it continues to face challenges related to rising costs and losses, necessitating careful management of its financial strategies moving forward.

(“Lotus Tech” or the “Company”), a leading global luxury electric vehicle maker, rang today’s opening bell at the Nasdaq stock exchange in New York City in celebration of its public listing. The Company’s American depositary shares (“ADSs”) commenced trading today on the Nasdaq Stock Market LLC (“Nasdaq”) under the ticker symbol “LOT” following the completion of its business combination on February 22, 2024, with L Catterton Asia Acquisition Corp (“L CAA”), a special purpose acquisition company (“SPAC”) formed by affiliates of L Catterton, a leading global consumer-focused investment firm. Lotus Technology Inc. CEO Feng Qingfeng was joined by members of the Lotus Technology Inc. and L Catterton teams in New York City to witness this important milestone, which will help Lotus Technology Inc. to further its mission of becoming an advanced, fully electric, intelligent, and sustainable luxury mobility provider under the brand’s Vision80 strategy. “This is an exciting moment for all of us at Lotus Technology Inc.,” said Mr. Feng. “I would like to extend my appreciation to our shareholders, partners, suppliers, our outstanding employees, and most importantly, our customers, for their support.

metadata.title_summary:
title: Lotus Technology Celebrates Public Listing
on Nasdaq 
summary: Lotus Technology Inc. (Lotus Tech), a leading luxury electric vehicle manufacturer, marked a significant milestone by going public on February 23, 2024, with its shares trading on the Nasdaq under the ticker "LOT." This event followed its merger with L Catterton Asia Acquisition Corp (LCAA), a SPAC linked to the global investment firm L Catterton. The public listing is a crucial step in Lotus Tech's ambition to become a leader in advanced, fully electric, and sustainable luxury mobility, aligning with its Vision80 strategy. CEO Feng Qingfeng celebrated the achievement alongside the L Catterton team, expressing gratitude to stakeholders and emphasizing the company's commitment to its heritage and future growth.

Chinta Bhagat, Co-CEO of LCAA, highlighted the potential for Lotus Tech to thrive in the expanding luxury electric vehicle market, aided by insights from their partnership with LVMH. The listing is expected to accelerate Lotus Tech's development of next-generation automotive technologies and expand its global distribution network from around 200 to over 300 stores by 2025. The company aims to be the first traditional luxury auto brand to offer a fully electric lineup by 2027, with plans to introduce additional high-performance models, including the Eletre hyper-SUV and Emeya hyper-GT, in the coming years. The celebratory bell-ringing ceremony was broadcasted from Times Square, featuring Lotus's signature battery electric vehicle models, allowing global employees to join in the festivities.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: Lotus Technology Inc. is proud to carry Lotus’s heritage into the future and to open a new chapter in the Lotus story with our public listing today.” Chinta Bhagat, Co-Chief Executive Officer of L Catterton Asia Acquisition Corp, said: “L Catterton Asia Acquisition Corp believes that Lotus Technology Inc. is ideally positioned to seize the tremendous opportunity in the fast-growing and underserved luxury electric vehicle market, leveraging our global consumer insights and strategic relationship with LVMH. Today’s public listing is a significant step in Lotus Technology Inc.’s future expansion, and L Catterton Asia Acquisition Corp is looking forward to continued collaboration with Lotus Technology Inc.’s talented management team.” The Nasdaq listing will support Lotus Technology Inc.’s development of next generation automobility technologies and the expected expansion of its global distribution network from around 200 to over 300 stores globally by 2025. Set to become the first traditional luxury auto brand to achieve a 100% electric product portfolio by 2027, Lotus Technology Inc. has launched a well-balanced portfolio of exceptionally performant models, including the Eletre hyper-SUV and Emeya hyper-GT, and plans to launch two additional luxury battery electric vehicles (BEV) over the next two years. The bell-ringing ceremony was broadcast on Nasdaq’s digital billboards in Times Square and livestreamed online, with Lotus Technology Inc. employees joining the celebration remotely from offices across the world. The brand’s signature battery electric vehicle models, Evija, Eletre, and Emeya, were also displayed in Times Square in front of the Nasdaq stock exchange.

Overall, Lotus Technology Inc. is navigating a complex landscape of rapid growth and financial challenges, while maintaining its legacy in automotive excellence and motorsports. How was Lotus Technology's performance in the first half of the year? Total revenue for the first half of 2024 was $398 million, representing a year-on-year increase of 206%. Lotus Technology's Average Selling Price (ASP) remained above $100,000, achieving growth far exceeding the industry and luxury car segment. Lotus Technology's gross margin for the first half of this year was 13%, compared to 5% in the same period of 2023.

In the first half of 2024, Lotus Technology achieved total product deliveries of 4,873 units, a 239% year-on-year increase, making it the fastest-growing brand among traditional luxury car brands. Global deliveries were balanced, with the four major markets (Europe, China, North America, and Rest of World) each contributing 20-30% of deliveries.

In the first half of 2024, as Lotus Technology began collaborating with several leading automotive brands by providing intelligent driving solutions and technology development services, technology-related revenue started to expand. With the continued increase in cumulative deliveries of lifestyle vehicles equipped with advanced hardware, the potential for generating paid software subscription revenue continues to grow.

Lotus Technology launched the Colin Chapman Bespoke Service, offering personalized options, designer customization, limited editions, and exclusive customization to meet the growing demands of its customers. The delivery of customized models is expected to further enhance the brand's luxury positioning and benefit Lotus Technology's long-term profit margins.

Any such recognized gain would be subject to the Default Passive Foreign Investment Company (PFIC) Regime (discussed below). However, it is difficult to predict whether, in what form, and with what effective date, final Treasury Regulations under Section 1291(f) of the Internal Revenue Code and any such other Passive Foreign Investment Company (PFIC) rules may be adopted and how any such Treasury Regulations would apply. metadata.title_summary:
title: PFIC Considerations of the Mergers 
summary: The summary discusses the tax implications for U.S. Holders of LCAA Securities in light of the Mergers, which may be classified as a reorganization. Despite this classification, U.S. Holders may still need to recognize gains for federal income tax purposes due to LCAA's status as a Passive Foreign Investment Company (PFIC). Under Section 1291(f) of the Internal Revenue Code, individuals transferring stock of a PFIC must acknowledge any gains from such transfers, irrespective of other tax provisions. Although final regulations under Section 1291(f) have not been issued, proposed regulations with a retroactive effective date suggest that U.S. Holders of LCAA Class A Ordinary Shares will need to recognize gains from the Mergers. Furthermore, U.S. Holders did not make a timely election to treat LCAA as a "qualified electing fund," which complicates their tax situation. The proposed regulations regarding LCAA Public Warrants are unclear, particularly regarding gain recognition when exchanging these for LTC Warrants. Additionally, the specifics of the final Treasury Regulations under Section 1291(f) and other PFIC rules remain uncertain, prompting U.S. Holders to seek advice from tax professionals to navigate these complexities and understand their implications.

NEW YORK, Jan. 14, 2025 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) -- Lotus Technology Inc. (“Lotus” or the “Company”), a leading global intelligent and luxury mobility provider, today announced its preliminary vehicle delivery results for the full year of 2024. Lotus Technology delivered 12,065 vehicles in 2024, representing a year-on-year increase of over 70% and boasting the top growth rate among leading luxury brands. China contributed approximately 25% of the total global deliveries in 2024. In 2025, Lotus Technology aims to increase global deliveries by 20% with a 25-28% contribution from China. Lotus Technology also successfully reclaimed the proprietary rights of the LOTUS roundel, LOTUS word mark, and the Chinese character of Lotus ("莲花") in China, achieving consistency in terms of its trademark across the world. Note 1: Invoiced deliveries, including commissioned deliveries in the US market. The vehicle delivery numbers presented above are derived from reports submitted by Lotus Technology's business units. These numbers may differ slightly from the vehicle delivery numbers recorded in the financial accounting and reporting systems announced or to be announced by Lotus Technology. Note 2: Including Ferrari, Porsche, Aston Martin, Bentley, Rolls Royce, and McLaren.

summary: The financial overview of Lotus Technology Inc. highlights significant growth and challenges across various metrics for the periods ending June 30, 2024, compared to the same periods in 2023. The bar chart analysis indicates a remarkable increase in financial figures, with quarterly revenues rising from **$130 million** in 1H23 to **$398 million** in 1H24, and from **$110 million** in 2Q23 to **$225 million** in 2Q24. Deliveries also surged, with 4,873 units delivered in 1H24, marking a **239%** year-over-year increase.

Despite these positive trends in revenue and deliveries, the company faced escalating costs, leading to increased operating losses and net losses. The operating loss expanded to **$438 million** in 1H24, and the net loss reached **$460 million**, reflecting ongoing investments and operational challenges. The adjusted EBITDA also showed a decline, indicating that while revenues grew, the company is still navigating significant financial hurdles.

In terms of assets and liabilities, total assets increased from **$1.58 billion** to **$2.51 billion**, driven by growth in current assets. However, total liabilities also rose, resulting in a narrower shareholders' deficit, which improved from **$(571 million)** to **$(202 million)**.

Additionally, the company is focused on strategic growth, as evidenced by its ambitious production targets, aiming for over **30,000 units** by 2026, and a gross profit margin exceeding **20%**. The vehicle lineup includes exclusive models like the Emeya x Blossom Enchantment and the Evija x Fittipaldi, emphasizing Lotus' commitment to luxury and performance.

Overall, while Lotus Technology Inc. demonstrates robust growth potential and a strong market presence, it continues to face challenges related to rising costs and losses, necessitating careful management of its financial strategies moving forward.

- **Table Summary:** This visual showcases the key milestones and achievements of Lotus Technology Inc. across its history, from 1957 to 2023, highlighting specific innovations, victories, and recognitions in motorsport and luxury design. It also provides a summary of Lotus Technology Inc.'s accolades, including championship wins, Grand Prix victories, and prestigious awards.
- **Context:** The context emphasizes Lotus Technology Inc.’s legacy as a leader in motorsport innovation and luxury category recognition, underlining its transformation and status as a globally recognized brand.
- **Special Notes:** All years are in chronological order, and specific achievements are tied to models or events. The laurels summarize Lotus Technology Inc.'s accomplishments in motorsport (e.g., F1 Constructors’ World Championships) and Le Mans victories.

[Row Level]
- **Row 1:** In 1957, the Lotus Eleven achieved its "First Le Mans Win" in the 750cc Index Class, cementing Lotus Technology Inc.'s presence in endurance racing.
- **Row 2:** In 1960, the Lotus Type 18 secured its "First Grand Prix Win" at Monte Carlo, marking Lotus Technology Inc.'s success in Formula 1 racing.
- **Row 3:** In 1963, the Lotus Type 25 achieved a double milestone by winning the "First Formula 1 Constructors' Championship" as well as the "First Formula 1 Drivers' Championship," showcasing the excellence of the car and team.
- **Row 4:** In 1965, the Lotus Type 38 claimed "First INDY 500 Victory," becoming the first rear-engine car to win this prestigious event, revolutionizing the race landscape.
- **Row 5:** In 1978, the Lotus Sunbeam won the "First World Rally Constructors' Championship," demonstrating Lotus Technology Inc.'s versatility in both on-road and rally formats.
- **Row 6:** In 2019, Lotus Technology Inc. was recognized as the "Luxury Brand of the Year" at the prestigious Luxury Briefing Awards, celebrating its influence in the premium automotive industry.
- **Row 7:** In 2020, the Lotus Evija secured the "2020 MUSE Global Design Awards," highlighting its groundbreaking innovation in design.
- **Row 8:** In 2023, the Lotus Eletre was named the "2023 GQ SUV of the Year," reflecting its impact in the SUV market as a luxury electric vehicle.
- **Row 9:** In 2024, Lotus Technology Inc. earned the "Carwow Brand Listing of Lotus," establishing its brand impact and visibility for that year.

- Lotus Technology Inc. has won **7 FIA Formula 1 Constructors' World Championships** and **6 FIA Formula 1 Drivers' World Championships**, demonstrating dominance in F1 racing.
- Lotus Technology Inc. boasts an impressive **81 FIA Formula 1 Grand Prix Wins**, along with **9 Le Mans Wins** in class, testament to its endurance racing legacy.
- Lotus Technology Inc. has achieved **1 Indianapolis 500 Grand Prix Win** and **1 FIA World Rally Constructors' Championship**, highlighting its innovations across multiple motorsport disciplines.

metadata.title_summary:
title: Commercial Rationale 
summary: Lotus Tech is emerging as a pioneering player in the sustainable luxury battery electric vehicle (BEV) market, leveraging its rich racing heritage and over seventy years of technological expertise to drive innovation and performance. The company operates under an asset-light model, benefiting from the Geely ecosystem, and is dedicated to achieving a fully electric product lineup and carbon neutrality by 2038. Its luxury retail strategy integrates premium physical locations with a digital-first approach to enhance customer engagement.

The management team is well-prepared for public operations, supported by strong corporate governance and a favorable financial outlook compared to industry peers. The merger with LCAA is positioned as a strategic move, with terms deemed fair for shareholders, who have options to remain invested, sell, or redeem their shares. Significant shareholder support has been secured, with 78.59% of Lotus Tech's shares committed to the merger.

The LCAA Board, composed mainly of independent directors, has thoroughly evaluated the merger terms and found them to be the most advantageous option after considering alternative opportunities. However, the merger faces several risks, including uncertainties in financial performance, profitability challenges, intense competition, potential loss of key personnel, macroeconomic factors, and the need for shareholder approval. Additional concerns include the possibility of litigation, the impact of exclusivity provisions, and the complexities of transitioning to a publicly traded entity.

Despite these challenges, the LCAA Board believes that the potential benefits of the merger outweigh the risks, affirming that the Business Combination aligns with the best interests of both LCAA and its shareholders.

metadata.title_summary:
title: Commercial Rationale 
summary: Lotus Tech is strategically positioning itself as a pioneer in the sustainable luxury battery electric vehicle (BEV) market, leveraging its racing heritage and innovative engineering to capture growth in attractive price segments and key regions. The company, backed by Geely's ecosystem, operates on an asset-light model and is committed to achieving a fully-electric product lineup and carbon neutrality by 2038. Its digital-first, omni-channel sales approach enhances the luxury retail experience.

As Lotus Tech prepares for its public debut through a merger with LCAA, it is supported by a seasoned management team and robust corporate governance. The merger terms have been deemed fair, with significant shareholder backing already secured, including a six-month lock-up agreement for certain major shareholders. The transaction values Lotus Tech at $5.5 billion, aiming to create long-term value for LCAA shareholders.

However, the merger faces various risks and uncertainties, including potential underperformance in financial results, competition in the luxury BEV market, and reliance on key personnel. Economic fluctuations, shareholder approvals, and the completion deadline of March 15, 2024, also pose challenges. The LCAA Board has evaluated these risks and believes the benefits of the merger outweigh the drawbacks, ultimately concluding that the Business Combination is in the best interests of its shareholders. Despite potential conflicts of interest among some LCAA officers and directors, the Board remains committed to advancing the merger, which is expected to close smoothly given the current shareholder support.

The plan outlines eligibility criteria, types of awards, vesting schedules, and administrative processes, with Mr. Qingfeng Feng appointed as the plan administrator. He is responsible for selecting eligible participants, determining award types and quantities, and establishing terms for each grant. The Award Agreement details the specific conditions associated with each share, including duration, provisions for employment termination, and the authority to amend or rescind awards.

Participants, including employees, directors, and consultants, must adhere to a vesting schedule set by the administrator, which dictates when they earn full rights to their awards. The plan allows for option grants, with an exercise price determined by the administrator and a maximum exercise period of ten years. Generally, awards cannot be transferred, except under specific conditions outlined in the plan or award agreement.

The 2022 Share Incentive Plan is intended to last for ten years, subject to earlier termination by the board of directors, which retains the authority to amend or suspend the plan, although certain changes require shareholder approval. Importantly, any modifications made by the administrator cannot adversely affect previously issued awards unless done in good faith.

title: 25. SHARE-BASED COMPENSATION 
summary: In September 2022, the Company's shareholders approved the 2022 Share Incentive Plan, designed to attract and retain top talent while incentivizing employees, directors, and consultants to support the Group's business success. This plan permits the issuance of options for a maximum of 51,550,661 ordinary shares. 

During the six-month period ending June 30, 2024, the Company granted a total of 6,184,901 options, a significant increase compared to the 2,691,667 options granted in the same period in 2023. Notably, in 2024, 64,229 options were awarded to non-employee service providers for operational consultation and business development, marking the first issuance of such options, as none were granted for these services in 2023. Additionally, 5,768 options were allocated to directors or officers in 2024, whereas no options were awarded to this group in 2023.

The share options granted under this plan have a contractual term of ten years and feature varying vesting terms, which require service periods ranging from none to three years for the options to vest. This structured approach aims to align the interests of the participants with the long-term goals of the Company, thereby enhancing overall business performance.
